volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Ghost (Section 4.4.978b - Section 4.5.1031b)

sections.label: Section 4.4.978b
sections.original: Quadraginta etiam dedit huic quae essent pignori ?
sections.translation: Has he given forty minae , too , to this person , to be as a deposit ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.979
sections.translation: Nor yet do I say so .

sections.label: Section 4.4.979b
sections.translation: Ah me ! you ’ ve proved my ruin !

sections.label: Section 4.4.979c
sections.translation: Aye , and he has proved the ruin of his father .

sections.label: Section 4.4.980
sections.translation: You prophesy the truth ! I could wish it false !

sections.label: Section 4.4.980b
sections.translation: A friend of his father , I suppose ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.981
sections.original: Eu edepol patrem eius miserum praedicas .
sections.translation: Ah me ! Upon my faith , you do pronounce him to be a wretched father .

sections.label: Section 4.4.981b
sections.translation: Why really , this is nothing at all — thirty minae , in comparison with the other expenses he has incurred in good living .

sections.label: Section 4.4.983
sections.translation: He has ruined his father .

sections.label: Section 4.4.983b
sections.translation: There ’ s one servant there a very great scoundrel , Tranio by name ; he could even waste the revenue of a Hercules [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.4.985
sections.original: edepol ne me eiús patris mísere miseret , qui cum istaec sciet
sections.translation: On my word , I ’ m sadly distrest for his father ; for when he comes to know that things have gone on thus , a hot coal will be scorching his breast , poor man .

sections.label: Section 4.4.987
sections.translation: If , indeed , this is the truth .

sections.label: Section 4.4.987b
sections.original: Quid meream , quam ob rem mentiar ?
sections.translation: What am I to gain , that I should tell a lie ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.988
sections.translation: ( Knocks again at the door .) Hallo , you ! is any one coming to open this door ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.988b
sections.original: Quid istas pultas , ubi nemo intus est ?
sections.translation: Why do you knock in this way , when there ’ s no one in the house ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.989
sections.original: alio credo comissatum abiisse . abeamus nunciam
sections.translation: I fancy that he ’ s gone elsewhere to carouse . Now then , let ’ s begone .

sections.label: Section 4.4.991
sections.translation: What , my lad , are you off then ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.991b
sections.translation: Liberty ’ s the overcoat for your back [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.4.992
sections.original: mihi , nisi ut erum metuam et curem , nihil est qui tergum tegam .—
sections.translation: Nothing have I with which to cover my back , except to pay respect and service to my master .

sections.label: Section 4.5.993
sections.original: Perii hercle , quid opust verbis ? ut verba audio ,
sections.translation: By my troth , I am undone ! What need is there of talking ? According to the words I have heard , I surely haven ’ t lately voyaged hence to Aegypt ,

sections.label: Section 4.5.995
sections.original: sed etiam in terras solas orasque ultumas
sections.translation: but even to some desolate land and the most remote shores have I been borne about , so much am I at a loss to know where I now am . But I shall soon know ; for see , here ’ s the person of whom my son bought the house . ( Enter SIMO .)

sections.label: Section 4.5.998
sections.translation: What are you about ?

sections.label: Section 4.5.998b
sections.translation: I ’ m coming home from the Forum .

sections.label: Section 4.5.999
sections.original: Numquid processit ad forum hodie novi ?
sections.translation: Has anything new been going on at the Forum to-day ?

sections.label: Section 4.5.1000
sections.translation: Why yes .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1000b
sections.translation: What is it , pray ?

sections.label: Section 4.5.1000c
sections.translation: I saw a dead man being carried to burial .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1000d
sections.translation: Dear me !

sections.label: Section 4.5.1001
sections.original: Novom unum : vidi mortuom efferri foras .
sections.translation: that is something new !

sections.label: Section 4.5.1002
sections.translation: They said that he had been alive but just before .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1002b
sections.translation: Woe to that head of yours for your nonsense !

sections.label: Section 4.5.1003
sections.original: Quid tu otiosus res novas requiritas ?
sections.translation: Why are you , thus idling about , enquiring after the news ?

sections.label: Section 4.5.1004
sections.translation: Because I ’ ve just arrived from abroad .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1004b
sections.translation: I ’ m engaged out to dine : don ’ t suppose I shall invite you [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1006
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I don ’ t want .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1006b
sections.translation: But , to-morrow , unless any person invites me first , I ’ ll even dine with you .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1008
sections.original: Ne istuc quidem edepol postulo . nisi quid magis
sections.translation: I ’ faith , and that , too , I don ’ t want . Unless you are engaged with something of greater importance , lend me your attention .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1009b
sections.translation: By all means .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1010
sections.translation: You have received , as far as I understand , forty minae of Philolaches .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1011b
sections.translation: Never a coin , so far as I know .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1012
sections.translation: What ? Not from my servant Tranio ?

sections.label: Section 4.5.1012b
sections.translation: Much less is that the case .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1013
sections.translation: Which he gave you by way of deposit ?

sections.label: Section 4.5.1013b
sections.translation: What are you dreaming about ?

sections.label: Section 4.5.1014
sections.original: Egone ? at quidem tu , qui istoc te speras modo
sections.translation: What , I ? Why , really , ’ tis yourself , who hope that , by dissembling in this manner ,

sections.label: Section 4.5.1015
sections.translation: you ’ ll be able to make void this bargain .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1016
sections.translation: Why , what do you mean ?

sections.label: Section 4.5.1016b
sections.original: Quod me apsente hic tecum filius
sections.translation: The business that , in my absence , my son transacted with you here .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1017b
sections.translation: How did your son ,

sections.label: Section 4.5.1018_1020
sections.original: dum tu hinc abes , negoti ? quidnam aut quo die ?
sections.translation: in your absence , transact any business with me ? What pray , or on what day ?

sections.label: Section 4.5.1021
sections.translation: I owe you eighty minae of silver .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1022
sections.original: Non mihi quidem hercle . verum , si debes , cedo .
sections.translation: Not to me , indeed , upon my faith ; but still , if you do owe them , give them me . Faith must be kept . Don ’ t be attempting to deny it .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1024
sections.original: Profecto non negabo debere , et dabo ;
sections.translation: Assuredly , I shall not deny that I owe them ; and I shall pay them .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1025
sections.original: tu cave quadraginta accepisse hinc te neges .
sections.translation: Do you take care how you deny that you received the forty from him .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1026bb
sections.translation: Troth now , prithee , look this way at me , and answer me .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1027
sections.original: Te velle uxorem aiebat tuo nato dare ,
sections.translation: He said that you were wishful to give a wife to your son ; for that reason , he said that you intended building on your own premises .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1029
sections.translation: I , intended building here ?

sections.label: Section 4.5.1029b
sections.translation: So he told me .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1030
sections.original: Ei mihi , disperii . vocis non habeo satis .
sections.translation: Ah me ! I ’ m ruined outright ! I ’ ve hardly any voice left [*] . Neighbours , I ’ m undone , ruined quite !

sections.label: Section 4.5.1031b
sections.translation: Has Tranio been causing any confusion ?
